title: Ensure that the --service-account-extend-token-expiration parameter is set to false

input_row.title: Ensure that the --service-account-extend-token-expiration parameter is set to false
input_row.description: By default Kubernetes extends service account token lifetimes to one year to aid in transition from the legacy token settings.
input_row.rationale: This default setting is not ideal for security as it ignores other settings related to maximum token lifetime and means that a lost or stolen credential could be valid for an extended period of time. Impact: Disabling this setting means that the service account token expiry set in the cluster will be enforced, and service account tokens will expire at the end of that time frame.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --service-account-extend-token-expiration argument is set to false.
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --service-account-extend-token-expiration parameter to false. --service-account-extend-token-expiration=false Default Value: By default, this parameter is set to true References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube- apiserver/
input_row.impact: Disabling this setting means that the service account token expiry set in the cluster will be enforced, and service account tokens will expire at the end of that time frame.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_service_account_extend_token_expiration_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver configuration to verify the value of the `--service-account-extend-token-expiration` flag.
- For clusters using static pod manifests (most kubeadm and self-hosted setups), inspect the `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` file for the presence and value of the `--service-account-extend-token-expiration` argument.
- Alternatively, use `kubectl get pod -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o yaml` to retrieve the running pod spec and check the command/args section for the flag.
- Pass Criteria: The flag `--service-account-extend-token-expiration` is explicitly set to `false`.
- Fail Criteria: The flag is absent (defaults to `true`) or set to `true`.
- Example kubectl command:
  ```
  kubectl get pod -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath="{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}"
  ```
  or
  ```
  kubectl get pod -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o json | grep -- '--service-account-extend-token-expiration'
  ```
- The automated check parses the command/args array for the kube-apiserver container and confirms that `--service-account-extend-token-expiration=false` is present.
